reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly encodes permutations of {1,...,n} as `Bijective` maps from `Finset.Icc 1 n` to itself. `Nat.dist` on ℕ equals |i-j|, so `Nat.dist i j = 1` accurately captures |i-j|=1, and `Nat.dist (pi i) (pi j) ≤ 2` captures |π(i)-π(j)| ≤ 2. The quantification `∀ i j : Finset.Icc 1 n` matches "for all i,j in {1,...,n}". The `.ncard` gives the correct cardinality. The conclusion `∀ n ≥ 2, (sum) = putnam_2015_b5_solution` simultaneously captures both "does not depend on n" (it equals a fixed constant) and "find its value" (that constant is 4). The signs and indices in the alternating sum P(n+5) - P(n+4) - P(n+3) + P(n) match the informal statement exactly. Using ℤ for the arithmetic avoids truncation issues. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
